title: Chris Richards ‘ready’ for World Cup opener in key injury boost – NBC Los Angeles
original_text:
Chris Richards ‘ready’ for World Cup opener in key injury boost – NBC Los Angeles
The U.S. men's national team may have an important injury boost days before its World Cup opener.
Speaking to the media on Wednesday, defender Chris Richards said he's "ready" for the first tournament game vs. Paraguay on Friday after rehabbing a sprained ankle.
"I'm ready," Richards told reporters. "I mean, it's the World Cup so I'm going to make myself ready regardless. I'm feeling good. Maybe a little swollen, but nothing tape can't help."
The 26-year-old injured his ankle late in the season with Premier League club Crystal Palace and was in a race against time to be fit. Though not fully 100%, him playing increases the USMNT's chances of going as far as possible.
He did not feature in the two pre-World Cup friendlies vs. Senegal and Germany, with Mark McKenzie and Miles Robinson getting the nods instead alongside veteran Tim Ream.
MORE USMNT COVERAGE
Richards, who missed out on the 2022 World Cup due to injury, opened up on how past scares reemerged.
"When I first came out with the injury, I was pretty devastated and I was honestly fearing the worst," he said. "But I kind of forced myself back on the pitch to prove to myself that it was doable...And that's what I've been doing in 24 hours, been doing recovery, been doing whatever it takes to be available for this first game."
Richards says he doesn't feel limited movement wise, but it'll be up to manager Mauricio Pochettino to make the official call.
If he doesn't play in the opener, he'll have another week to prepare with Australia next on Friday, June 19.
USMNT-Paraguay kicks off at 9 p.m. ET/6 p.m. PT at Sofi Stadium in Los Angeles.
1:33
USMNT's Chris Richards Shares the Story Behind His MLK Tattoo
The defender for Crystal Palace and the U.S. Men’s National Team shares why he chose to get Martin Luther King, Jr., tattooed on his arm.
